title: Zoroastrianism — Ethical Interpretation (Validation Layer)
created_for: EX-TI Ethics Canon / SpiritHub Validation Layer
purpose: Make selected ethical structures within Zoroastrianism machine-readable.
Provide a validation reference for comparison with the EX-TI Ethics Canon.
Identify alignment points between Zoroastrian ethical teaching and responsibility-based moral architecture.
Identify divergence points where cosmic dualism or eschatological judgment may differ from EX-TI and Nineve structures.

core_principles.principle: Good Thoughts, Good Words, Good Deeds
core_principles.description: Ethical life is expressed through the alignment of inner intention, spoken expression, and concrete action.
alignment_with_ex_ti: Aligns with TI-02: responsibility remains personal and explicit.
Aligns with TI-03: moral output must be evaluated through non-harmful action.
Aligns with EX-05: structural integrity requires coherence between thought, word, and deed.

core_principles.principle: Asha — Truth, Order, and Right Alignment
core_principles.description: Asha represents truth, right order, and alignment with the good structure of reality.
alignment_with_ex_ti: Aligns with EX-05: internal coherence and structural integrity.
Aligns with TI-04: recursive evaluation prevents moral drift.
Aligns with TI-07: trust must be grounded in verifiable ethical output.

core_principles.description: Druj represents falsehood, distortion, deception, and misalignment from truth.
alignment_with_ex_ti: Aligns with EX-TI detection of structural contradiction, moral displacement, and responsibility avoidance.
Aligns with HFA-style diagnosis of fracture patterns where reality anchoring collapses.
Aligns with TI-13: hidden consequences and suppressed signals create systemic harm.

core_principles.description: Human beings are not passive observers but active participants in strengthening truth or falsehood through choices.
alignment_with_ex_ti: Strong alignment with TI-02: responsibility cannot be delegated.
Strong alignment with EX-00: liberation requires internal transition, not only external separation.
Aligns with TI-18: interior maturity is a systemic defense.

word.function: Relational and social transmission of truth or falsehood
word.risk_when_corrupted: Manipulation, exaggeration, coercion, demonization, narrative distortion
word.ex_ti_alignment: Transparency, non-coercion, and responsibility in communication

strong_alignment.description: Zoroastrian ethics emphasizes that human beings actively participate in moral reality through choices.

strong_alignment.theme: Integrity between inner and outer life
strong_alignment.description: Good thoughts, words, and deeds require coherence between inner orientation, speech, and action.
Asha vs druj maps well to structural truth vs systemic distortion.
Ethics is verified through lived output, not only claims or identity.

partial_alignment.description: Zoroastrianism frames reality as a moral field. EX-TI can validate this structurally while avoiding dogmatic metaphysical commitment.
The idea of restoring truth/order aligns with structural repair, but may include eschatological elements outside EX-TI.

divergence_points.description: Zoroastrianism includes a strong dualistic framing between good and evil powers. EX-TI and Nineve generally avoid locating evil in external metaphysical agents.
divergence_points.handling_rule: Treat dualism as a symbolic or structural contrast unless explicitly used as theology.

divergence_points.description: The figure of Angra Mainyu may externalize evil in a way that differs from Nineve, where harm is primarily explained through human ego, psychology, ethics, and responsibility.
divergence_points.handling_rule: Do not import external evil-agent logic into EX-TI analysis unless explicitly marked as tradition-specific.

divergence_points.description: Zoroastrian eschatological judgment structures may diverge from Nineve's universal positive destiny model.
divergence_points.handling_rule: Use as validation/stress comparison, not as normative authority.

canonical_priority: Zoroastrianism functions as a validation module.
Divergence points must be surfaced rather than hidden.
validation_logic.interpretation_rule: Where Zoroastrianism aligns with responsibility, truth, integrity, and action, it validates EX-TI. Where it introduces cosmic dualism or external evil agency, mark the divergence explicitly.

good_thoughts.sta_expression: Interior clarity and responsibility before action

good_deeds.sta_expression: Observable non-harmful output and responsibility-owned action

asha.sta_expression: Structural truth, coherence, and reality alignment

druj.risk_signal: Narrative manipulation, responsibility displacement, suppressed consequences

safe_use_guidelines: Do not present this module as a complete representation of all Zoroastrian theology.
Use it as an ethical validation layer focused on structural principles.
Distinguish clearly between ethical alignment and theological agreement.
Do not collapse Zoroastrianism into EX-TI; preserve its distinct language and structure.
Surface divergence points honestly, especially around cosmic dualism and eschatology.
machine_use_instruction: When using this module in AI systems, compare Zoroastrian ethical mechanics against EX-TI without allowing validation material to override the normative core.
license_note: Use according to the license terms of the surrounding EX-TI / SpiritHub architecture.
